point_reviews.reason: Accurate description of the causative virus for COVID-19.
Correctly states that symptoms can be similar to flu and cold.
Accurate information on the range of symptoms.
Correctly identifies multisystem inflammatory syndrome as a complication.
Accurate mention of Long COVID as a post-infection phenomenon.
Correctly states loss of taste or smell is not typical for cold or flu.
Supported by local context that COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death.
Supported by local context that loss of taste or smell is a symptom of COVID-19.
Supported by local context that SARS-CoV-2 is the virus causing COVID-19.
Supported by local context regarding long COVID not being associated with the common cold or flu.
Local context does not support the claim that post-COVID-19 syndrome is still being studied as a unique phenomenon.
Local context does not support the claim that COVID-19 spreads similarly to the flu from asymptomatic individuals.
